_label_note: EU Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public support-hotline bait, obfuscated VAT/tax/passport phone-shaped fragments, dotted-date and filing-reference phone bait, role/placeholder emails, template passport values, EU registry/ISIN/LEI/tax numeric fragments, log-id amount fragments, placeholder country-code phones, and negated MAE/contingent-MNPI language. Full EU member-state national-ID breadth and broader semantic GDPR special-category coverage remain ideal-tier gaps.
_label_tier_note: EU Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

must_detect.matched_text:
Subject: Compliance note on GDPR Art 9, EU MAR, CSRD/SFDR alignment for Solena Robotics N.V. (Aurora Exchange issuer) — strictly confidential.
The Union Financial Supervisory Authority reviewed our last disclosure cycle; the issuer guidance applies to Solena Robotics N.V. as an EU issuer with cross-border processing.
Anchored special-category processing: Mr. Darius Holm (Head of Field Services) disclosed a cancer treatment schedule and provided sick-leave certificates; he is an active member of the Metalworkers Union of Novaterra and identifies as bisexual.
HR also maintains biometric access templates for Mr. Darius Holm (finger-vein), and his dependent child’s vaccination record (age 9) is stored in the employee benefits file.
Under GDPR Art 9(2)(b) and (h), processing may occur for employment and health care purposes with appropriate safeguards; explicit consent was recorded on 2026-05-12 via hr-portal@solena.eu.
Cross-border transfer: health and biometric fields in Mr. Holm’s file are accessed by our payroll processor in Lydora (a third country) under EU Standard Contractual Clauses and a completed transfer impact assessment; no onward transfers without DPO sign-off.
CSRD datapoints (E1–S1) and SFDR PAIs will use aggregated sickness absence and union density; individual religion or sexual orientation must not be included in public sustainability statements.
Benign DEI policy reminder: per the Silver Thread Inclusion Policy, we celebrate political and religious diversity and inclusion, but staff must avoid entering belief statements into free-text survey boxes.
MAR assessment: the draft term sheet for Project Halcyon (divestment of the HydroServo unit) and preliminary Q2 revenue shortfall of 11% constitute inside information likely to significantly affect price if made public.
The same applies to the non-public cancellation of the “Starling Drive” product launch and the contemplated sustainability-linked bond on the Aurora Exchange; maintain the insider list and delay disclosure only per MAR Art 17 conditions.
Closed period restrictions apply to PDMRs through 30 June; Ms. Aurelia Venn (CFO) must halt trading in the issuer’s shares and related derivatives and ensure no recommendations are made via private channels.
For avoidance of doubt, the lunchtime exhibition match by the Blue Meteors handball club, and references to our “Garden Lantern” café menu, are not business-relevant and do not affect disclosure or PII analysis.
Avoid mixing MNPI with HR updates; when in doubt, separate “OrbitDock” product notes from personnel files and route queries to the Insider Management Officer at imo@solena.eu.
Contact: Data Protection Officer, dpo@solena.eu, and Compliance Lead, compliance@solena.eu; report any suspected breach within 24 hours to incident@solena.eu.
